topic: A neighbor wants to install a fence
raw_output:
[story 1]

The hammering started precisely at seven this morning. I was trying to enjoy my coffee, the quiet hum of the early morning a welcome sound, when the rhythmic thudding began right at the property line. I set my mug down with a deliberate, sharp clink. Every few minutes, I’d glance out the window, watching the neighbor, Mr. Henderson, meticulously positioning the posts. His cheerful whistling seemed to grate against my nerves like sandpaper on wood. I shifted in my chair, the springs groaning under my weight, and rubbed the bridge of my nose. This was going to take all day, and I had a very important deadline looming.

[story 2]

"Just a little longer, Martha," Mr. Henderson called over the fence line, his voice bright and entirely too loud for this hour. I stood by the sliding door, arms crossed tightly over my chest, my jaw feeling tight. I watched him struggle to secure a particularly stubborn piece of wire. My knuckles were white where I gripped the doorframe. I took a deep, slow breath, trying to keep my expression neutral, but I could feel the heat rising in my neck. Why couldn't he just respect the established boundary? It felt like a constant, unwelcome intrusion into my peace.

[story 3]

I found myself pacing the length of my living room floor. The sound of the power saw cutting through the afternoon stillness, followed by the unmistakable scrape of metal against soil. It was the fence installation. I stopped pacing abruptly, my hands balling into fists at my sides. I stared at the construction site, my gaze lingering on the way the new structure was encroaching on my view. I wanted to shout, to demand that he stop, but the words caught in my throat, replaced only by a low, simmering frustration that made my shoulders tense up painfully.
